meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve SaaS Blindness
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between coding and business. Learn how to implement advanced analytics to track real user behavior and validate your first SaaS project.
content.answer_block: To overcome SaaS blindness, implement event-based analytics immediately. By integrating tracking into your core user flows—signup, activation, and churn—you transform raw code into actionable business intelligence. Using Antigravity and Claude Code, you can automate this telemetry, ensuring every feature launch provides verifiable data on user engagement and product-market fit.
content.intro:
You have the code, the repository, and the ambition, yet your dashboard remains a graveyard of zeros. The transition from 'writing code' to 'building a SaaS' isn't just about syntax; it is about visibility. Without data, you are flying blind, guessing what your users want while your hard work goes unnoticed in the void of the internet.

This case study solves that. We are moving beyond simple page views to architecting a telemetry system that tracks intent. By leveraging modern AI-driven toolchains, you will turn your first project into a data-backed product, proving to potential employers and users alike that you understand the lifecycle of a real-world application.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Identify the one action that defines value for your specific SaaS. This prevents vanity metric overload.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest 5 high-impact user events to track based on my core features. Prioritize actions that indicate user activation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my project structure and list the essential user events for a baseline analytics implementation.'

steps.title: Automated Instrumentation Setup
steps.description: Use AI to inject tracking code across your frontend and backend without manual boilerplate bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular analytics service wrapper that supports event batching, error handling, and environment-based configuration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a new analytics service file in /src/services/analytics.ts and integrate it into the main app entry point.'

steps.title: User Identity Mapping
steps.description: Associate events with unique user IDs to track the full customer journey from anonymous visitor to paying subscriber.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my authentication middleware to trigger an 'identify' call every time a user logs in or registers, ensuring consistent tracking.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Update auth middleware to integrate user identification into the analytics service.'

steps.title: Funnel Visualization Logic
steps.description: Create automated event triggers for your primary conversion funnel to identify where users drop off.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a systematic way to track funnel stages (Landing -> Sign-up -> First Action) using the analytics service I created.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Instrument the signup and onboarding components with trackEvent calls for each funnel step.'

steps.title: Telemetry Verification
steps.description: Validate that events are firing correctly in your development environment before pushing to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test script that simulates user activity and logs the payload of every analytics event triggered to the console.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Run the simulation script to verify analytics payload delivery in the staging environment.'

pro_tips: Never track everything; track what impacts revenue. If an event doesn't inform a product decision, delete it.
Always sanitize your event payloads to ensure PII (Personally Identifiable Information) never hits your analytics provider.
Use server-side tracking for critical events to prevent ad-blockers from skewing your conversion data.
Set up automated alerts for 'zero-event' days to detect breaking changes in your tracking pipeline immediately.
Correlate your analytics with error logs to see if drop-offs are caused by UX issues or technical bugs.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, wait 48 hours to collect baseline data, then identify your 'leakiest' funnel stage and run an A/B test to improve conversion.
content.conversion_block: Don't let your code go unseen. Mastering the data layer is the final step in becoming a full-stack SaaS engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
